steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: one team collected arthropods with a method, another gathered them with bug vacuums. The blank must supply the collection method plus any needed punctuation, and 'for instance' signals an example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — sweep nets, for instance;: names the method, uses a comma to set off the parenthetical 'for instance,' then a semicolon to properly join the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice B — sweep nets; for instance,: puts the semicolon before 'for instance,' separating it from the clause it modifies and leaving the second independent clause joined only by a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — sweep nets, for instance,: sets off the parenthetical 'for instance' with commas and uses only a comma between the two independent clauses, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — sweep nets for instance: no punctuation at all between the independent clauses; runs them together as a fused sentence. ✗
That is the correct answer.
